message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Since the beginning of the COVID-19 pandemic, researchers and health authorities have sought to identify the different parameters that govern their infection and death cycles, in order to be able to make better decisions. In particular, a series of reproduction number estimation models have been presented, with different practical results.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>This article aims to present an effective and efficient model for estimating the Reproduction Number and to discuss the impacts of sub-notification on these calculations.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The concept of Moving Average Method with Initial value (MAMI) is used, as well as a model for R
                    <jats:sub>t</jats:sub>
                    , the Reproduction Number, is derived from experimental data. The models are applied to real data and their performance is presented.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Analyses on R
                    <jats:sub>t</jats:sub>
                    and sub-notification effects for Germany, Italy, Sweden, United Kingdom, South Korea, and the State of New York are presented to show the performance of the methods here introduced.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    We show that, with relatively simple mathematical tools, it is possible to obtain reliable values for time-dependent, incubation period-independent Reproduction Numbers (R
                    <jats:sub>t</jats:sub>
                    ). We also demonstrate that the impact of sub-notification is relatively low, after the initial phase of the epidemic cycle has passed.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: COVID-19: Time-Dependent Effective Reproduction Number and Sub-notification Effect Estimation Modeling

reference.unstructured: De Carvalho, EA , De Carvalho, RA . Identification of Patterns in Epidemic Cycles and Methods for Estimating Their Duration: COVID-19 Case Study. MedRxiv (2020). Published 2020 Jul 15. doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.07.13.20153080
